text:
Once upon a time there was a wise old owl who lived in a big tree. Every day he'd fly out from his home high up high in the forest canopy, exploring every nook and cranny.

One particular morning the wise owl flew out, but instead of exploring he found a small, tiny frog sleeping in a moonlit pond. The frog was so cozy curled up next to a reed next to the water that even the moonlight seemed to glow.

The owl hopped down carefully not to wake the frog. He listened intently.

That's when he heard the frog's tired voice say, "I could stay here all day!"

The wise owl huffed softly.

"I know I could keep you company," the wise owl said.

So the owl hovered over the little frog, offering a friendly earwig to nibble on.

The frog woke up with a big stretch, blinked, and said, "Well, that might be interesting." 

The owl chuckled softly.
